task: Turn brightness to the min value.

steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the home screen of an Android device. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Phone, Messages, Chrome, and another Gmail icon in the dock. The Settings app is not visible on this main home screen page.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open the Settings app → | Step 2: Navigate to Display settings | Step 3: Adjust the brightness level to the minimum value, task complete

[Decision]
To adjust the system brightness to the minimum value, I need to access the device's settings. Since the Settings app is not present on the current home screen shortcuts, I will use a system-level open action to launch the Settings app directly.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The current screen is the Settings app main menu. Visible options include Network & internet, Connected devices, Apps, Notifications, Battery (100%), Storage, and Sound & vibration. The "Display" option is not currently visible on the screen.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Settings app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Display settings → | Step 3: Adjust the brightness level to the minimum value, task complete

[Decision]
The current step in the plan requires navigating to Display settings. Since the "Display" option is not in the currently visible list of settings items (which ends at Sound & vibration), I need to scroll down the page to find it.

[Memory]
Target: Set brightness to minimum. Current state: Settings app opened, looking for Display menu.

[Observation]
The screen displays the Settings menu after scrolling. The "Display" option is now clearly visible, with subtext mentioning "Dark theme, font size, brightness". Other visible options include Sound & vibration, Wallpaper & style, Accessibility, Security, Privacy, Location, Safety & emergency, and Passwords & accounts.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Settings app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Display settings ✓ | Step 3: Adjust the brightness level to the minimum value →

[Decision]
The current plan step requires accessing the Display settings to adjust the brightness. Since the "Display" option is now visible on the screen, I need to tap on it to enter the specific menu where the brightness slider or adjustment controls are located.

[Memory]
Target: Set brightness to minimum. Current state: "Display" menu located and selected. Next expected screen: Display settings page containing brightness controls.

[Observation]
The screen displays the "Display" settings page. Under the "Brightness level" section, the current value is explicitly shown as "0%". Other options like Lock screen, Screen timeout, Dark theme, and Screen saver are also visible.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Settings app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Display settings ✓ | Step 3: Adjust the brightness level to the minimum value ✓

[Decision]
The goal is to turn the brightness to the minimum value. Based on the observation, the "Brightness level" is already at "0%", which is the minimum possible value. Since the objective has been met and no further adjustments are needed, I can conclude that the task is complete.

[Memory]
Target: Set brightness to minimum. Current state: Display settings page reached, brightness level confirmed at 0% (minimum). Task completed successfully.
